name: Five-Grain Soda Bread
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons Soft margarine or vegetable oil
1 3/4 cups Buttermilk (or lemon juice mixed with milk, or vinegar mixed with milk)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix vinegar or lemon juice with enough milk to make 1 3/4 cups buttermilk.
2. If using powdered skim milk, mix almost 2/3 cup powder and enough water to make 1 3/4 cups of milk, then add the lemon juice and more water until the mixture is just right.
3. If not all flours are available, use what you have on hand to make about 3 1/4 cups total. You can also add a quarter cup of wheat germ or bran to 3 cups of all-purpose flour.
4. Sugar may be omitted if using more than 2 tablespoons of raisins (or finely chopped dates).
5. Combine dry ingredients in a large bowl.
6. Cut in margarine or oil, then stir in raisins (if using) and add buttermilk to form a soft dough.
7. Knead lightly on a floured surface until smooth. Shape into a round about 2 1/2 inches thick, cut an X about 1/4 inch deep on top, or divide into two smaller loaves.
8. Bake at 350°F for about 1 hour, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Note: The original recipe text contains additional information and notes that are not part of the main recipe instructions.
